nuscale_ch01_0072.title: Combined License Information Items
nuscale_ch01_0072.summary: This section explains the interface requirements between the NuScale Power Plant’s certified design and the site-specific design provided in the combined license (COL) application. It distinguishes between conceptual design information (CDI) for non-certified portions and COL information items, which are site-specific details the COL applicant must address. Additionally, it notes that COL applicants must provide a list of any departures from the certified design.

nuscale_ch01_0031.title: Plant Cooling Water Systems
nuscale_ch01_0031.summary: The plant cooling water systems in the NuScale design include several nonsafety-related systems that support plant operations. These systems consist of the reactor component cooling water system (RCCWS), reactor and spent fuel pool cooling systems, and the circulating water system, which collectively manage heat removal from various plant components and transfer it to the site cooling water system or cooling towers. These systems ensure efficient cooling for both operational and maintenance needs.

nuscale_ch01_0015.title: General Plant Description
nuscale_ch01_0015.summary: This section provides an overview of the NuScale plant design, summarizing key systems such as safety features, power conversion, fuel handling, cooling, and waste management. It explains the distinction between certified and site-specific design elements, noting that some conceptual design information is included for context, and clarifies how security-related information is handled.

nuscale_ch01_0038.title: Main Control Room
nuscale_ch01_0038.summary: The Main Control Room (MCR) is located below grade in the Control Room Building (CRB) and includes various support spaces such as offices, conference rooms, and equipment rooms. It houses control panels for all installed NuScale Power Modules (NPMs), allowing reactor operators to monitor and control multiple NPMs using digital control systems that ensure independence between safety and nonsafety systems. The MCR provides comprehensive monitoring, alarms, and controls for plant operations, with additional display stations for shared systems and diagnostics.

nuscale_ch01_0033.title: General Arrangement of Major Structures and Equipment
nuscale_ch01_0033.summary: This section describes the general arrangement of major structures and equipment at a NuScale Power Plant, focusing on the central Reactor Building (RXB). The RXB is a large, reinforced concrete structure designed for safety and radiation protection, housing the NuScale Power Modules (NPMs) in a common reactor pool with individual bays arranged in two rows. The section also references detailed figures and notes that some buildings, like administration and warehouse facilities, are outside the scope of the safety report.

nuscale_ch01_0032.title: Radioactive Waste Management System
nuscale_ch01_0032.summary: The section describes the NuScale Power Plant's radioactive waste management system, which handles liquid, gaseous, and solid wastes, with detailed discussions in Chapter 11 of the report. It also outlines the general arrangement of major structures, focusing on the Reactor Building (RXB), a reinforced concrete, Seismic Category I structure designed for safety and radiation protection, housing the nuclear power modules (NPMs) in a central reactor pool.

edges.description: Section A outlines the interface requirements and boundaries between the certified design and site-specific design, which directly inform the conceptual site layout that must accommodate systems like the plant cooling water systems described in Section B.

Section A specifies the interface requirements and boundaries between the certified design and site-specific design, while Section B provides the general description of the overall facility, including the structures and systems referenced in Section A.

Section A outlines the interface requirements and boundaries between the certified design and site-specific design, while Section B provides specific details about the location and associated spaces of the Main Control Room within those boundaries, thus Section B specifies part of the site layout referenced in Section A.
Section A provides specific details about the plant cooling water systems and power conversion system, which are components broadly described in Section B's general plant description.

Section A describes the plant cooling water systems and their physical arrangement, which informs the overall site layout that must also accommodate the Main Control Room and its associated spaces as described in Section B.
Section A provides a general overview of the plant layout, while Section B specifies the location and associated spaces of the Main Control Room as part of that overall facility description.

Section A describes the general arrangement and layout of major structures and equipment, while Section B specifies how these arrangements interface with site-specific design requirements in the combined license application, both referencing Figure 1.2-2 to connect certified and site-specific design boundaries.

Section A's general arrangement of major structures and equipment includes the overall site layout, which encompasses the placement of systems like those described in Section B, such as the plant cooling water systems that are part of the power conversion system.

Section A provides specific details and visual layout information about the arrangement of major structures and equipment, which elaborates on the general facility description summarized in Section B.

Section A references Figure 1.2-2 for the site layout, which Section B specifies as showing the boundaries between certified and site-specific design, thus Section A's discussion of layout relies on the boundary information detailed in Section B.

Both Section A and Section B reference figures (1.2-2 and 1.2-3) that illustrate the physical arrangement of major plant systems, which together inform the conceptual site layout by showing the placement of key structures and equipment.

Section A provides specific details about the radioactive waste management system and references the general plant layout, which is broadly described in Section B as part of the overall facility description.
